chapters.summary: Covers overview of cell structure, prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, cell membrane models (Fluid Mosaic), cell wall, endomembrane system (ER, Golgi, Lysosomes, Vacuoles), mitochondria, plastids, ribosomes, cytoskeleton, nucleus, and microbodies.

keyConcepts.title: Fluid Mosaic Model
keyConcepts.description: Proposed by Singer and Nicolson (1972) to explain cell membrane structure. Lipids are arranged as a bilayer with embedded integral and peripheral proteins.

keyConcepts.title: Endomembrane System
keyConcepts.description: Group of membranes and organelles that work together to modify, package, and transport lipids and proteins. Includes ER, Golgi apparatus, lysosomes, and vacuoles.

formulas.name: Note on Calculations
formulas.description: Prokaryotes: 1-10 micrometers, Eukaryotes: 10-100 micrometers. Ribosomes in prokaryotes are 70S; in eukaryotes, they are 80S (cytoplasm) and 70S (organelles).

videos.title: Cell: The Unit of Life One Shot for NEET

practiceResources.title: NEET NCERT Exemplar MCQ Practice Sheet

quizzes.question: Which of the following cell organelles is responsible for extracting energy from carbohydrates to form ATP?
quizzes.explanation: Mitochondria are the sites of aerobic respiration. They generate ATP through cellular respiration, extracting energy from carbohydrates and lipids (powerhouse of the cell).

quizzes.question: The fluid mosaic model of cell membrane was proposed by:
quizzes.explanation: The fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane, which describes the cell membrane as a tapestry of molecules, was proposed by S.J. Singer and G.L. Nicolson in 1972.
